comment_text.sn35.125:1.2: At AN 1.254 and AN 6.126 he is called Uggata, which I adopt for clarity over text’s Ugga here.

root_text.sn35.125:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vajjīsu viharati hatthigāme.
root_text.sn35.125:1.2: Atha kho uggo gahapati hatthigāmako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho uggo gahapati hatthigāmako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.125:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme no parinibbāyanti?
root_text.sn35.125:1.4: Ko pana, bhante, hetu ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyantī”ti?
root_text.sn35.125:1.6: “Ayaṁ kho, gahapati, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyantī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.125:0.3: In the Land of the Vajjis
translation_text.sn35.125:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Vajjis at Elephant Village.
translation_text.sn35.125:1.2: Then the householder Uggata of Elephant Village went up to the Buddha, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn35.125:1.3: “What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some sentient beings are not fully extinguished in this very life?
translation_text.sn35.125:1.4: What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some sentient beings are fully extinguished in this very life?” …
translation_text.sn35.125:1.5: (Tell in full as in the previous discourse.)
